verification_method: Square wall clock with 12-hour Arabic numerals. Hour hand clearly between 2 and 3 (closer to 2). Minute hand pointing at the 4 marker, which corresponds to 20 minutes. Hour position is consistent with 2:20 (hour hand should be 1/3 between 2 and 3). High confidence.

source: Wikimedia Commons File:Bentima Mechanical Wall Clock.jpg
verification_method: Brass decorative wall clock with Arabic numerals. Minute hand clearly points at the 8 (=40 minutes). Hour hand is close to 3 but a touch before, consistent with 2:40 (hour hand should be 2/3 between 2 and 3 at :40). High confidence.

Certina wristwatch. Minute hand points directly at the 9 marker (=45 minutes). Hour hand is also at the 9 area, just past 9 going toward 10, which is consistent with 9:45 (hour hand should be 3/4 between 9 and 10). High confidence.

Red-framed Infinity wall clock with Arabic numerals. Minute hand points clearly at the 10 marker (=50 minutes). Hour hand is just before the 7, very close to it, consistent with 6:50 (hour hand should be 5/6 between 6 and 7). High confidence.
Reguladora wall clock at Sintra station. Minute hand points at the 2 marker (=10 minutes). Hour hand is just past the 10, consistent with 10:10. Filename also encodes the time. High confidence.

E.A. Combs London wall clock. Minute hand points clearly at the 9 marker (=45 minutes). Hour hand is just before the 7, almost touching it, consistent with 6:45 (hour hand should be 3/4 between 6 and 7). High confidence.

Kensington Station 1879 ornamental clock. Minute hand points at the 10 marker (=50 minutes). Hour hand is just before the 3, consistent with 2:50 (hour hand should be 5/6 between 2 and 3). High confidence.
WIKA EN 837-1 bar gauge, scale 0 to 25 bar. Black needle points between 15 and 20, clearly past the midpoint and aimed at the 18 mark. Reading is approximately 18 bar. High confidence.

Submarine instrument: Schaffer and Budenberg pressure gauge labeled mW.S. (meters water column), scale 0 to 120. Black needle points squarely at the 100 mark on the right side of the dial. High confidence.

source: Wikimedia Commons File:102 degrees in Tallahassee.jpg
verification_method: Kia automotive tachometer labeled x1000rpm with scale 0 to 8. Red needle points clearly at the 1 marker, indicating 1000 RPM. Vehicle is in Drive but stationary (speedometer reads 0 MPH, which is shown separately on the digital display). High confidence.

source: Wikimedia Commons File:1913 Ford Model T Speedster speedometer.JPG
verification_method: 1913 Ford Model T Johns-Manville speedometer. Scale runs 5, 10, 15... 60 mph. The black needle is pointing directly at the 5 marker on the left side of the dial. Odometer reads 04126 total. High confidence on needle position at 5 mph.

Antique 1890s aneroid barometer. Numeric scale visible: 720, 730, 740, 750, 760, 770, 780, 790. Black needle points directly between 770 and 780 mark, closer to 770. Reading approximately 770 mmHg. Weather labels (Variable, Bau Temps, Bau Fixe) confirm orientation. High confidence.

Hansaetic Hamburg aneroid barometer with dual scales: outer mmHg 720-800 and inner hPa 960-1070. Black needle points to the boundary between Change and Fair, which corresponds to approximately 1020 hPa (also ~765 mmHg on outer scale). High confidence.
